error: noun. The state, quality, or condition of being wrong.
ope: interjection. An exclamation of surprise; oops.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
peer: verb. To look with difficulty, or as if searching for something.
perter: adjective. comparative form of pert: more pert
pet: noun. An animal kept as a companion or otherwise for pleasure, rather than for some practical benefit or use.
poet: noun. A person who writes poems.
pore: noun. A tiny opening in the skin.
port: noun. A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.
porter: noun. A person who carries luggage and related objects.
pot: noun. A flat - bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food, possibly excluding saucepans (see usage notes).
pree: verb. to heed, to take notice of or pay attention to
pro: noun. An advantage of something, especially when contrasted with its disadvantages (cons).
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
repo: noun. Clipping of repossession, most commonly of a vehicle, house or condominium.
report: verb. To relate details of (an event or incident); to recount, describe (something).
reporter: noun. Someone or something that reports.
repot: verb. To move (a growing plant) from one pot to a larger one to allow for further growth.
ret: verb. To prepare (flax, hemp etc.) for further processing by soaking, which facilitates separation of fibers from the woody parts of the
rete: noun. A network of blood vessels or nerves.
retro: adjective. Of, or relating to, the past, past times, or the way things were.
rope: noun. Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.
roper: noun. Agent noun of rope; one who uses a rope, especially one who throws a lariat or lasso.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rote: noun. Mechanical routine; a fixed, habitual, repetitive, or mechanical course of procedure.
tee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter T / t.
terror: noun. Intense dread, fright, or fear.
toe: noun. Each of the five digits on the end of the human foot.
top: noun. The highest or uppermost part of something.
tope: verb. To drink excessively; to get drunk.
toper: noun. Someone who drinks alcoholic beverages a lot; a drunkard.
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
tree: noun. A perennial woody plant taller and larger than a shrub with a wooden trunk and, at some distance from the ground, having leaves and
trop: noun. Alternative form of trope (“cantillation pattern”).
trope: noun. Something recurring across a genre or type of art or literature; a motif.
ert: verb. To incite; urge on; encourage.
opter: noun. One who opts, or makes a choice.
petro: noun. A Venezuelan cryptocurrency launched in 2018, supposed to be backed by the country's oil and mineral reserves.
por: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Portuguese.
porer: noun. One who pores, or studies closely.
porret: noun. A scallion; a leek or small onion.
prore: noun. The front part of a ship.
rort: noun. A scam or fraud, especially involving the misappropriation of public money or resources.
teer: verb. To stir, as a calico - printer 's sieve.
trp: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kokborok.
